---
title: "Water Sample Code"
output:
  html_notebook: default
  pdf_document: default
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

START CODE


adding a clearance rate column to the data set water_sample_data
```{r}
water_sample_data <- within(water_sample_data, clearance.rate <- ((volume/(1*time))*log(initial.conc/final.conc)))
print(water_sample_data)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(water_sample_data, aes(x = mussel.Weight, y = clearance.rate)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  xlab("Mussel Weight (g)") + ylab("Clearance Rate (mL/H)")
```
```{r}
ggplot(water_sample_data, aes(x = initial.conc, y = clearance.rate)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  xlab("Inital Algae Concentration (cells/mL)") + ylab("Clearance Rate (mL/H)")
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
summary_df_h0 <- water_sample_1 %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(hour.zero, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(hour.zero, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
```
```{r}
summary_df_h1 <- water_sample_1 %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(hour.one, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(hour.one, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
```
```{r}
summary_df_h2 <- water_sample_1 %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(hour.two, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(hour.two, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
```
```{r}
summary_df_h3 <- water_sample_1 %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(final.three, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(final.three, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
```
```{r}
summary_df <- bind_rows(
  summary_df_h0 %>% mutate(hour = 0),
  summary_df_h1 %>% mutate(hour = 1),
  summary_df_h2 %>% mutate(hour = 2),
  summary_df_h3 %>% mutate(hour = 3)
)
```
```{r}
as.data.frame(summary_df)
```


```{r}
plot_summary <- function(money,c){ 
  summary_df %>%
    filter(Treatment == money) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = hour, y = mean_val)) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", colour = c ) +
    ggtitle(money, "Algae starting concentration over time") +
    xlab("Time (hr)") + ylab("Algae concentration (cells/ml)") + 
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_val - se_val, ymax = mean_val + se_val), 
                                                                             width = 0.1,
                                                                             color = "black",
                                                                             linewidth = 0.5)
}
```

```{r}
plot_low <- plot_summary("Low", "red")
plot_medium <- plot_summary("Medium", "blue")
plot_high <- plot_summary("High", "green")
plot_highest <- plot_summary("Highest", "violet")
```
```{r}
library(cowplot)
```
```{r}
plot_grid(plot_low, plot_medium, plot_high, plot_highest)
```


```{r}
 ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = hour, y = mean_val, colour = Treatment)) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE ) +
    ggtitle("Algae starting concentration over time") +
    xlab("Time (hr)") + ylab("Algae concentration (cells/ml") + 
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_val - se_val, ymax = mean_val + se_val), 
                                                                             width = 0.1,
                                                                             color = "black",
                                                                             linewidth = 0.5)
```
```{r}
CR_OT <- summary_df %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  transmute("hr 0-1" = ((900/(1*1))*log(mean_val[hour == 0]/ mean_val[hour == 1])),
                "hr 1-2" = ((900/(1*1))*log(mean_val[hour == 1]/ mean_val[hour == 2])),
                "hr 2-3" = ((900/(1*1))*log(mean_val[hour == 2]/ mean_val[hour == 3])))

```
```{r}
CR_OT <- unique(CR_OT)
```
```{r}
library(tidyr)
```
```{r}
CR_OT2 <- CR_OT %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("hr"),
    names_to = "interval",
    values_to = "clearance.rate"
  )
```
```{r}
CR_OT2 <- CR_OT2 %>%
  mutate(interval = recode(interval,
                               "hr 0-1" = 1,
                               "hr 1-2" = 2,
                               "hr 2-3" = 3))
```



```{r}
ggplot(CR_OT2, aes(x = interval, y = clearance.rate, colour = Treatment)) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
    ggtitle("Clearance rate over time") +
    xlab("Time interval") + ylab("Clearance rate (ml/hr)")
```
```{r}
model <- lm(clearance.rate ~ mussel.Weight * Treatment, data = water_sample_1)
anova(model)
summary(model)
```
```{r}
model <- lm(clearance.rate ~ mussel.Weight + Treatment, data = water_sample_1)
anova(model)
summary(model)
```
```{r}
ggplot(water_sample_1, aes(x = mussel.Weight, y = clearance.rate, colour = Treatment)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)
```

```{r}
water_sample_1 <- within(water_sample_1, clearance.rate.overall <- ((volume/(1*time))*log(hour.zero/final.three)))
print(water_sample_1)
```
```{r}
water_sample_1 <- within(water_sample_1, clearance.rate.interval_1 <- ((volume/(1*1))*log(hour.zero/hour.one)))
water_sample_1 <- within(water_sample_1, clearance.rate.interval_2 <- ((volume/(1*1))*log(hour.one/hour.two)))
water_sample_1 <- within(water_sample_1, clearance.rate.interval_3 <- ((volume/(1*1))*log(hour.two/final.three))/mussel.Weight)
```
```{r}
CR_interval1 <- water_sample_1 %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(clearance.rate.interval_1, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(clearance.rate.interval_1, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
  
```
```{r}
CR_interval2 <- water_sample_1 %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(clearance.rate.interval_2, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(clearance.rate.interval_2, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
```

```{r}
CR_interval3 <- water_sample_1 %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(clearance.rate.interval_3, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(clearance.rate.interval_3, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
```
```{r}
print(water_sample_1)
```


```{r}
summary_cr <- bind_rows(
  CR_interval1 %>% mutate(interval = 1),
  CR_interval2 %>% mutate(interval = 2),
  CR_interval3 %>% mutate(interval = 3)
)
```
```{r}
ggplot(summary_cr, aes(x = interval, y = mean_val, colour = Treatment)) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_line() +
    xlab("hour interval") + ylab("Clearnace Rate (ml/hr)") + 
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_val - se_val, ymax = mean_val + se_val), 
                                                                             width = 0.1,
                                                                             color = "black",
                                                                             linewidth = 0.5)
```
```{r}
ggplot(summary_cr, aes(x = interval, y = mean_val, colour = Treatment)) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_line() +
    xlab("Interval") + ylab("Clearance Rate (ml/hr)")
```
```{r}
ggplot(water_sample_1, aes(x = mussel.Weight, y = log(clearance.rate), colour = Treatment)) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_line() +
    xlab("Mussel weight(g)") + ylab("log of Clearance Rate")
```
```{r}

```


```{r}
sum_cr <- water_sample_1%>%
  group_by(Treatment)%>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(clearance.rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(clearance.rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
  
```

```{r}
m3 <- lm(log(clearance.rate) ~ log(mussel.Weight) + hour.zero, data = water_sample_1)
anova(m3)
summary(m3)

```

```{r}
plot(m3)
```

```{r}
m1 <- lm(log(clearance.rate) ~ log(mussel.Weight) + Treatment, data = water_sample_1)
anova(m1)
summary(m1)
```
```{r}
ggplot(summary_df_h0, aes(x = Treatment, y = mean_val, fill = Treatment)) + 
  geom_col() + 
  ylab("Initial algae concentration (cells/ml)") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_val - se_val, ymax = mean_val + se_val), 
                                                                             width = 0.1,
                                                                             color = "black",
                                                                             linewidth = 0.5)
```
```{r}
m4 <- aov(hour.zero ~ Treatment, data = water_sample_1)

summary(m4)
```
```{r}
m4
```
```{r}
TukeyHSD(m4)
```
```{r}
m2 <- lm(log(clearance.rate) ~ log(mussel.Weight), data = water_sample_1)

summary(m2)
```
```{r}
m5 <- lm(log(clearance.rate) ~ Treatment, data = water_sample_1)
summary(m5)
```
```{r}
library(emmeans)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
set.seed(42)
dat <- tibble(
  animal_id = factor(1:12),
  concentration = factor(rep(c("C", "L", "M", "H"),each = 3),
                         levels = c("C", "L", "M", "H")),
  weight = runif(12,0.5, 3.0),
  clearance_rate = rnorm(12, mean = 5, sd = 1.5)
)%>%
  mutate(
    log_cr = log(clearance_rate),
    log_weight = log(weight)
  )
```
```{r}
dat1 <- tibble(
  animal_id = water_sample_1$mussel.Id,
  concentration = water_sample_1$Treatment,
  weight = water_sample_1$mussel.Weight,
  clearance_rate = water_sample_1$clearance.rate
)%>%
  mutate(
    log_cr = log(clearance_rate),
    log_weight = log(weight)
  )
```
```{r}
# ── Model 1: full — weight x concentration interaction ───────────────────────
mod1 <- lm(log_cr ~ log_weight * concentration, data = dat1)
 
# ── Model 2: additive — weight and concentration as main effects only ─────────
mod2 <- lm(log_cr ~ log_weight + concentration, data = dat1)
 
# ── Model 3: concentration only ───────────────────────────────────────────────
mod3 <- lm(log_cr ~ concentration, data = dat1)
 
# ── Model 4: weight only ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
mod4 <- lm(log_cr ~ log_weight, data = dat1)
 
# ── Model 5: null ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
mod5 <- lm(log_cr ~ 1, data = dat1)
```

```{r}
# ── AIC overview ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
AIC(mod1, mod2, mod3, mod4, mod5)
 
# ── Nested LRT comparisons ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
 
# Test weight x concentration interaction: is effect of weight on
# clearance rate different between concentration treatments?
anova(mod1, mod2)
 
# Test weight term: does size explain variance after accounting for concentration?
anova(mod2, mod3)
 
# Test concentration term: does treatment explain variance after weight?
anova(mod2, mod4)
```

```{r}
# Update mod_best based on selection results above
mod_best <- mod2   # placeholder — update as needed
 
summary(mod_best)
anova(mod_best)
 
# ── Check assumptions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(mod_best)
 
# ── Post-hoc pairwise comparisons between concentration levels ────────────────
emmeans(mod_best, pairwise ~ concentration, adjust = "tukey")
 
# ── Plot clearance rate by concentration ─────────────────────────────────────
ggplot(dat1, aes(x = concentration, y = exp(log_cr))) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 4, colour = "red") +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar",
               width = 0.15, colour = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    x = "Algae concentration treatment",
    y = "Clearance rate (mL/hr)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)
```

```{r}
# Range of sample sizes to test
n_range <- seq(6, 60, by = 6)
 
power_results <- map_dbl(n_range, function(n) {
  # Simulate 500 datasets of size n with similar effect size to your data
  sims <- replicate(500, {
    sim_dat <- tibble(
      concentration = factor(rep(c("C", "L", "M", "H"), length.out = n),
                             levels = c("C", "L", "M", "H")),
      log_weight    = rnorm(n, mean = mean(dat1$log_weight), sd = sd(dat1$log_weight)),
      log_cr        = rnorm(n, mean = mean(dat1$log_cr), sd = sd(dat1$log_cr))
    )
    mod_sim <- lm(log_cr ~ log_weight + concentration, data = sim_dat)
    anova(mod_sim)["concentration", "Pr(>F)"] < 0.05
  })
  mean(sims)
})
 
tibble(n = n_range, power = power_results) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n, y = power)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, colour = "#2A9D8F") +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", colour = "#E63946") +
  annotate("text", x = max(n_range), y = 0.82, label = "80% power",
           hjust = 1, colour = "#E63946") +
  labs(x = "Sample size (n)", y = "Power") +
  theme_classic()
```




END CODE

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Ctrl+Alt+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Ctrl+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
